candidates.title: The Sensitivity Floor: Why Absorbed and Non-Absorbed Features Are Both Insensitive
candidates.summary: Sensitivity requires BOTH high specificity AND high reliability. Absorbed features have low specificity (decoder directions close to neighbors). Non-absorbed features in sparse regions have low reliability (low activation frequency). Neither can achieve the sensitivity threshold. Q2 and Q4 are structurally impossible. The positive correlation (r=0.59) is a linear approximation of a U-shaped relationship.
hypotheses: H-SF1: Q2+Q4 remain empty (<5%) even at N>200
H-SF2: S(A) = aA^2 + bA + c with a > 0 (U-shape)
H-SF3: Frequency mediates r(absorption, sensitivity) - partial r < 0.3
H-SF4: Geometric density mediates r(absorption, sensitivity) - partial r < 0.3
candidates.pilot_focus: Larger sample (200 features) to confirm Q2/Q4 emptiness; 30 min pilot
candidates.key_reference: iter_008 pilot: Q4=0/43, r=0.59, ratio=1.0; Korznikov 2026 Sanity Check
candidates.novelty_risk: High (8/10) - Sensitivity floor is genuinely novel; no prior work connects specificity/reliability to absorption
candidates.risk_mitigation: Pilot is fast (30 min); falsifiable; alternatives available if SF falsified
candidates.iter_008_relationship: Built on all iter_008 falsifications: Q4 emptiness, r=0.59 positive correlation, ratio=1.0
candidates.stagnation_alert: Zero experiments for 2+ iterations - MUST execute pilot first

candidates.title: Sensitivity-First: Sensitivity Failures Alone Explain Sanity Check
candidates.summary: Sensitivity failures (NOT absorption) are the primary driver of Sanity Check. Q3 dominates at 65% of features. Absorption adds no predictive value after controlling for sensitivity. 85% of features are low-sensitivity regardless of absorption.
hypotheses: H-A: Low-sensitivity features (Q3) show steering not different from random baseline
H-B: Absorption does NOT predict steering after controlling for sensitivity
candidates.pilot_focus: Steering on 28 Q3 features at beta=5; compare to random baseline
candidates.novelty_risk: Moderate (6/10) - Sensitivity-first explanation not claimed in prior work
candidates.risk_mitigation: Simplest explanation; directly testable; pilot is fast (15 min)
candidates.iter_008_relationship: Directly uses Q3 features from iter_008 pilot; tests whether sensitivity alone explains Sanity Check

candidates.title: Geometric Density as Common Cause of Absorption and Sensitivity Failure
candidates.summary: Both failure modes share geometric density as common cause. Dense regions cause decoder overlap (absorption) AND vague activation patterns (low sensitivity). Density mediates r=0.59.
hypotheses: H-B1: Absorbed features have higher geometric density than non-absorbed
candidates.pilot_focus: Compute geometric density for 100 features; test r(density, absorption)
candidates.key_reference: r=0.59 positive correlation; Empiricist perspective; OrtSAE orthogonality
candidates.novelty_risk: Moderate (6/10) - Density as mediating variable not previously proposed
candidates.risk_mitigation: Density is directly measurable; generates unified intervention
candidates.iter_008_relationship: Explains the positive correlation (r=0.59) that falsified compound failure independence
candidates.pivot_trigger: H-SF3 and H-SF4 both fail: neither frequency nor density mediates

candidates.title: Layer-wise UAS Saturation: Measurement Path Forward
candidates.summary: UAS saturation is layer-dependent, not universal. Some layers (4, 6, 10) show UAS variance while layer 8 shows saturation. This determines where absorption CAN be measured and whether Sensitivity Floor is layer-specific.
hypotheses: H-C1: UAS variance differs across layers (some show std > 0.05)
H-C2: At non-saturated layers, sensitivity correlates with UAS (r > 0.4)
candidates.pilot_focus: Measure UAS at layers 4, 8, 10 (30 features each); compute mean and std per layer
candidates.key_reference: UAS=1.0 saturation at layer 8 (iter_007); Empiricist + Contrarian perspectives
candidates.novelty_risk: Moderate (6/10) - Layer-wise saturation mapping is descriptive but essential
candidates.risk_mitigation: Addresses contrarian objection; determines measurement path for all experiments
candidates.iter_008_relationship: Addresses layer-specificity concern from Contrarian; measurement prerequisite for Sensitivity Floor
candidates.pivot_trigger: Contrarian layer-specificity objection needs resolution; run alongside pilot if time permits

candidates.title: Steering Diffusion Explains Beta=20 Reversal
candidates.summary: The beta=20 reversal (low-absorption > high-absorption, p=0.015) is caused by steering diffusion. High-absorption features have decoder directions that overlap with MORE neighbors. At high beta, steering activates multiple neighbors simultaneously, diluting the effect.
hypotheses: H-D1: Absorbed features have higher decoder-neighbor overlap
H-D2: Diffusion is non-linear in beta; null at beta<=10, effect at beta=20
candidates.pilot_focus: Measure decoder-neighbor overlap for 50 features; fit diffusion model to iter_004 data
candidates.key_reference: iter_004 beta=20 reversal (p=0.015); H6 falsified ratio=1.0
candidates.novelty_risk: Low (reanalysis of beta=20) but mechanism is novel given H6 falsification
candidates.risk_mitigation: Explains an unexplained finding; consistent with H6 falsification
candidates.iter_008_relationship: Addresses beta=20 reversal that H6 falsification left unexplained
candidates.pivot_trigger: Secondary mechanism; run after primary experiments confirm Sensitivity Floor

dropped_from_prior.reason: H5 FALSIFIED (r=0.59, not independent), Q4=0 (not empty), H6 FALSIFIED (ratio=1.0). The compound failure hypothesis assumed independence but pilot shows positive correlation.
dropped_from_prior.action: Dropped as front-runner. cand_sensitivity_floor (sensitivity floor) explains the positive correlation through complementary mechanisms.

dropped_from_prior.reason: H1-R FALSIFIED: r=+0.36, not negative protective. Earlier r=-0.786 did not replicate across layers 4, 8, 10.
dropped_from_prior.action: Dropped permanently. The coherence-abs relationship is unstable and not reliably protective.

dropped_from_prior.reason: H6 FALSIFIED: L2 norm ratio = 1.0, not > 1.1. Decoder L2 norm saturation is not the mechanism for beta=20 reversal.
dropped_from_prior.action: Dropped as backup. cand_steering_diffusion provides an alternative mechanism (neighbor overlap) that is consistent with ratio=1.0.

evaluation_framework.absorption_detection: Chanin et al. 2024 first-letter protocol (tau_fs=0.03, tau_ps=0.025, tau_pa=0.4)
evaluation_framework.sensitivity_measurement: Tian et al. 2025 paraphrase AUC protocol
evaluation_framework.steering_protocol: Add beta * W_dec[feature] to residual stream at layer 8
evaluation_framework.geometric_density: Mean cosine similarity to k=20 nearest neighbors in activation space
evaluation_framework.frequency_measurement: Fraction of tokens with feature active (sae_features > 0)

h5_result.interpretation: Absorption and sensitivity are POSITIVELY correlated, not independent. Common cause model preferred.

h6_result.interpretation: No decoder L2 norm difference between absorption groups. Saturation mechanism not supported.

h1r_result.interpretation: Earlier r=-0.786 did not replicate. Coherence-abs relationship is unstable.

quadrant_counts.interpretation: Q4 empty - sensitivity failures are near-universal. 100% of features are low-sensitivity.

stagnation_warning.action_required: Execute pilot experiments FIRST. Theoretical refinement without data is self-perpetuating stagnation.
